title: Assessing Lysozyme-Like Activity in Galleria mellonella Hemolymph using EnzChek Kit
author: Juan Jose Quispe Haro
materials: All steps are done in the dark.

steps.title: Surface sterilize the blades of a...
steps.instruction: Surface sterilize the blades of a pair of micro scissors or a scalpel by spraying them with 70% ethanol and allowing them to air dry.

steps.title: Gently squeeze the larva to collect...
steps.instruction: Gently squeeze the larva to collect the dripping hemolymph (aim for 30-100 µl per larva) into a pre-chilled tube containing 30 µl of ice-cold anticoagulant buffer. Optional: use 100 µl pipette to collect the last fraction of hemolymph

steps.title: Centrifuge the tubes at table top...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the tubes at table top centrifuge at 12000 rpm for 1 minute to separate cellular debris from the hemolymph. Optional: If centrifuging at 4 °C, you can use 4000 x g for 10 minutes.

steps.title: Cover the plate with a transparent...
steps.instruction: Cover the plate with a transparent lid or seal film, and then wrap in aluminum foil and incubate at 37 °C for 30 minutes.

steps.title: Add 50 μl of 50 μg/ml...
steps.instruction: Add 50 μl of 50 μg/ml DQ lysozyme substrate to all 8 tubes and mix.

steps.title: Measure the fluorescence intensity with a top-reading microplate reader
steps.instruction: Measure the fluorescence intensity with a top-reading microplate reader. Ex: 489 nm. Em: 523 nm

steps.title: Optional
steps.instruction: Optional: Measure every 5 minutes for 30 minutes total to get an average read value.

steps.title: Dispense 30 µl of anticoagulant buffer into 0
steps.instruction: Dispense 30 µl of anticoagulant buffer into 0.2 ml microfuge tubes, and register their respective mass (1 tube per larva).

steps.title: Take the overnight bacterial culture and...
steps.instruction: Take the overnight bacterial culture and transfer 2 ml into an Eppendorf tube

steps.title: Grow 3 ml of an overnight...
steps.instruction: Grow 3 ml of an overnight bacteria culture at 37 °C in an incubator with vigorous shaking (150-200 rpm)

steps.title: Centrifuge at no more that 1000...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at no more that 1000 rcf, for 10 min at 4 °C.

steps.title: Discard the supernatant and resuspend the...
steps.instruction: Discard the supernatant and resuspend the bacterial pellet in 1 ml of sterile PBS

steps.title: Measure the absorbance at 600 nm...
steps.instruction: Measure the absorbance at 600 nm and dilute the suspension so it contains about 10^6 cells/ml

steps.title: Select Galleria mellonela larvae in their...
steps.instruction: Select Galleria mellonela larvae in their 7th instar (usually, above 400 mg in weight) and distribute in Petri dishes as groups of 5 for each condition.

steps.title: Prepare a Hamilton syringe with 10...
steps.instruction: Prepare a Hamilton syringe with 10 µl of bacterial suspension or sterile PBS in case of control.

steps.title: Grab a larva by its sides...
steps.instruction: Grab a larva by its sides with the thumb and the index fingers and flip it upside down to expose its abdomen

steps.title: Localize the hind/left proleg of the...
steps.instruction: Localize the hind/left proleg of the larva and carefully insert the needle of the syringe about 5 mm into the body of the larva

steps.title: Inject the contents of the syringe...
steps.instruction: Inject the contents of the syringe slowly to prevent any leaking, and remove the syringe.

steps.title: Return the larva to their respective...
steps.instruction: Return the larva to their respective dish and repeat for all the larvae and conditions.

steps.title: Transfer the Petri dishes to an...
steps.instruction: Transfer the Petri dishes to an incubator and incubate the larvae for 0-6 hours at 37 °C

steps.title: Optional
steps.instruction: Optional: Anesthetize the larvae on ice for 5-10 minutes to minimize movement during hemolymph collection

steps.title: Surface sterilize the larvae by spraying...
steps.instruction: Surface sterilize the larvae by spraying them with 70% ethanol over a paper towel and allow them to air dry.

steps.title: Place the tube back on ice immediately after collection
steps.instruction: Place the tube back on ice immediately after collection.

steps.title: Repeat the collection steps for all the larvae
steps.instruction: Repeat the collection steps for all the larvae.

steps.title: With the blade, make a small...
steps.instruction: With the blade, make a small cut in a abdomen of the larva.

steps.title: Register the weight and add ice...
steps.instruction: Register the weight and add ice cold anticoagulant buffer to ensure the final ratio of hemolymph to buffer is 1:1

steps.title: Optional
steps.instruction: Optional: Carefully transfer aliquots of the clear supernatant (cell-free hemolymph) to a new pre-chilled tube.

steps.title: Optional
steps.instruction: Optional: Snap-freeze aliquots in liquid nitrogen and store at -20°C until ready for the next assay.

steps.title: In a black plastic 384 well...
steps.instruction: In a black plastic 384 well plate, add 30 μl of each mixture to three consecutive wells.

steps.title: Generate the standard curve by averaging...
steps.instruction: Generate the standard curve by averaging the fluorescence readings per concentration, and performing a linear regression.

steps.title: Transfer the tubes to an ice...
steps.instruction: Transfer the tubes to an ice tray and wait a couple of minutes so the contents of the tubes are ice-cold.

steps.title: Prepare 8 microtubes with 50 μl of 1x reaction buffer
steps.instruction: Prepare 8 microtubes with 50 μl of 1x reaction buffer

steps.title: Optional
steps.instruction: Optional: Flash freeze the tubes in liquid nitrogen and store at -20 °C until needed.

steps.title: Add 50 μl of 1000 U/ml...
steps.instruction: Add 50 μl of 1000 U/ml stock of lysozyme to the first tube. Mix well by pippetting

steps.title: Prepare 1:2 serial dilutions with the next 6 tubes tube
steps.instruction: Prepare 1:2 serial dilutions with the next 6 tubes tube. The last tube should remain with reaction buffer only.

steps.title: The following steps are done in...
steps.instruction: The following steps are done in the dark to avoid the formation of fluorescein conjugates.

steps.title: Pipette 15 μl of this mixture...
steps.instruction: Pipette 15 μl of this mixture into three consecutive wells of a black plastic 384-well plate.

steps.title: Mix 25 μl of CFH with...
steps.instruction: Mix 25 μl of CFH with 25 μl of 1x reaction buffer.

steps.title: Add 15 μl of 50 μg/ml...
steps.instruction: Add 15 μl of 50 μg/ml DQ lysozyme substrate to each well.

steps.title: Cover the plate with a transparent...
steps.instruction: Cover the plate with a transparent lid or seal film, and then wrap in aluminum foil and incubate at 37 °C for 30 minutes.

steps.title: Measure the fluorescence intensity with a top-reading microplate reader
steps.instruction: Measure the fluorescence intensity with a top-reading microplate reader. Ex: 489 nm. Em: 523 nm

steps.title: Optional
steps.instruction: Optional: Measure every 5 minutes for 30 minutes total to get an average read value.

steps.title: Pool the readings of each sample...
steps.instruction: Pool the readings of each sample set and average the fluorescence intensity reading.

steps.title: Use this value to calculate the...
steps.instruction: Use this value to calculate the lysozyme activity equivalent of the samples from the standard curve.
